title: Clinic Intake: Describe Pain Clearly
situation: At a clinic check-in, you need to explain pain location, duration, and intensity.
userGoal: Use simple symptom language and ask follow-up questions when you are unsure.
pressureQuestion: How long has this pain lasted and where is it strongest?
contextNotes: Language practice only, not medical advice.
safePhrases: It's strongest on the lower right side.
betterAnswer: The pain started about 12 hours ago. It's sharp on my lower right side, around 7 out of 10 right now.

title: Counseling Intake: Explain Panic Symptoms
situation: During first counseling intake, you need to describe panic episodes and communication needs.
userGoal: Describe symptoms and boundaries clearly without self-judgment.
pressureQuestion: What happens in your body when panic starts?
contextNotes: Language practice only, not mental health treatment.
safePhrases: My chest feels tight and my breathing gets fast.
Could we slow down for a moment so I can explain clearly?
betterAnswer: When it starts, my heart races, my hands shake, and I feel dizzy for about ten minutes. Speaking slowly helps me stay focused.
